channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Japanese court convicts Australian who says she was tricked into smuggling drugs
item.description: CHIBA, Japan (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday sentenced an Australian woman to six years in prison for smuggling amphetamines into the country, despite accepting her testimony that she was tricked as part of an online romance scam.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: A look at Hezbollah's plan to terrorize and invade northern Israel
item.description: Fox News Digital reviewed plans, and was shown video of a Hezbollah terrorist describing the group’s plan to kill or capture Israeli civilians in its planned invasion of the Galilee region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian forces battling insurgents north of strategic city of Hama
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syria said Wednesday its counteroffensive has pushed back insurgents attempting to advance to the strategic central city of Hama, while the insurgency says it captured more Syrian troops and Iran-backed militants in fierce battles.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippines says China's coast guard fired water cannons and sideswiped its patrol vessel
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Chinese coast guard vessels backed by navy ships fired powerful water cannons and blocked and sideswiped a Philippine patrol vessel on Wednesday in renewed aggression at a disputed shoal in the South China Sea, Philippine officials said.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Activists file second impeachment complaint against embattled Philippine Vice President Duterte
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A group led by left-wing activists filed a second impeachment complaint against the Philippine vice president on Wednesday over her alleged misuse of government funds and demanded that she be permanently barred from holding public office.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shock martial law edict in South Korea follows chaotic recent history: A look at the crucial context
item.description: It may seem that the wild predawn scenes in Seoul, with hundreds of armed troops and frantic lawmakers storming South Korea's parliament building after the president suddenly declared martial law, came out of nowhere.
item.dc:creator: Foster Klug, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senegalese artisans in the spotlight as they exhibit for the first time at a prestigious art event
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — For the artistic and cultural elites of Senegal, the monthlong Dakar Biennale of Contemporary African Arts is a celebratory moment.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Demonstrators again converge on Georgia's parliament after the country's EU bid was suspended
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Thousands of demonstrators in the Georgian capital converged on parliament again on Tuesday, venting outrage against the governing party's decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN human rights watchdog opens investigation into Venezuela presidential election
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — The top United Nations human rights watchdog on Tuesday ordered Venezuela to avoid destroying tally sheets and other electoral material as it investigates allegations that President Nicolás Maduro stole this summer's election. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes on Gaza hospital wound 3, Netanyahu vows 'iron fist' in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli drone strikes hit Kamal Adwan Hospital on Tuesday, wounding three medical staff at one of the few hospitals still partially operating in the northernmost part of Gaza, the facility’s director said. Dr. Hossam Abu Safiya said the drones were dropping bombs, spraying shrapnel at the hospital.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about South Korea's short-lived and chaotic period of martial law
item.description: South Korea’s President Yoon Suk Yeol is facing parliamentary moves to impeach him after he sent heavily armed forces into Seoul’s streets with his baffling and sudden declaration of martial law that harkened to past dictatorships.
item.dc:creator: Foster Klug, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An Israeli strike kills a person in Lebanon, further shaking the tenuous ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon’s Health Ministry and state media say Israeli forces have carried out several drone and artillery strikes in southern Lebanon, killing one person.
item.dc:creator: Bassam Hatoum And Sally Abou Aljoud, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean parliament blocks president's martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol attempted to declare martial law in the country for the first time in 45 years but was blocked by a parliament vote early Wednesday morning local time. He vowed to lift the order after hours of chaos and protests in the capital of Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: China responds to US chip sanctions, banning exports of key minerals
item.description: China has banned exports of some rare minerals to the United States as tensions over trade heat up after the U.S. issued new restrictions on exports of certain semiconductor chips and equipment to China earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The G20 has 'shock absorbers' to deal with Trump's return as US president, South Africa says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The Group of 20 has sufficient “shock absorbers” to function effectively if the incoming Donald Trump administration promotes an America First policy at the expense of international cooperation, South Africa's leader said Tuesday as his country took over the bloc's pre
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas and Fatah are near an agreement on who will oversee postwar Gaza
item.description: Palestinian officials say Fatah and Hamas are closing in on an agreement to appoint a committee of politically independent technocrats to administer the Gaza Strip after the war.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy And Josef Federman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Police in Georgia move again to disperse protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Police in Georgia's capital again moved to disperse thousands of demonstrators on Monday after over 200 people were detained during four previous nights of protests against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian tourist dies after being swept away by massive wave while doing yoga in Thailand
item.description: Russian tourist Kamilla Beliatskaya died on Friday after she was swept away by a large wave while doing yoga on a rock in Koh Samui, Thailand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration to announce $725M weapons package to Ukraine months before Trump is sworn in
item.description: The Biden administration is announcing a $725 million weapons package for Ukraine less than two months before President-elect Donald Trump takes office, a U.S. official says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Putin leader backs harsh crackdown as over 40 people hospitalized amid protests in Georgia
item.description: Dozens are hospitalized after massive protests broke out across Georgia following the pro-Putin government's move halting decadeslong talks on joining the EU amid increasing ties with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vanuatu calls on ICJ to recognize climate change's harm
item.description: Representatives for the island nation of Vanuatu called on the International Court of Justice this week to recognize the harm caused by climate change, the first remarks the United Nations court heard as it considers international obligations to address climate impacts.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan president speaks with US officials from Hawaii as China condemns visit
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te touched down in Hawaii over the weekend and spoke by phone with former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) as China slammed the U.S. for the visit and for selling weapons parts to Taipei. Hawaii Gov.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Biden travels to Africa where policies were ‘over-promised and under-delivered,' amid massive China expansion
item.description: President Biden visits Angola this week and is spending billions to get better supplies of electric vehicle battery metals, but the admin may have already been beaten by China's investments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China sentences journalist for espionage after he was detained while eating with Japanese diplomat
item.description: Chinese journalist Dong Yuyu was sentenced in a Beijing court to seven years in prison for espionage after meeting with a Japanese diplomat at a restaurant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Islamist rebels in Syria catch Assad, Putin, Iran regimes off guard giving US new mideast headache
item.description: Amid a rapid-fire collapse of Syrian, Hezbollah, Russian and Iranian forces in northern Syria, questions are surfacing about U.S. policy toward the emerging new order in the war-ravaged Syrian Arab Republic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Imprisoned Kremlin critic convicted again, receives 3-year sentence for opposing war in Ukraine
item.description: Imprisoned Kremlin critic Alexei Gorinov was convicted again on Friday and sentenced to three years in prison for opposing Russia's ongoing war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British lawmakers give initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British lawmakers gave initial approval on Friday to a bill to help terminally ill adults end their lives in England and Wales, following an impassioned debate that saw people sharing personal stories of loss and suffering.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation
